review_notes: The functions accurately map to Kubernetes RBAC controls, which are relevant for enforcing role-based access control as per NIST AC-3(7). The naming conventions are mostly followed, but a minor adjustment was made for consistency. The functions are descriptive and indicate clear pass/fail conditions.
naming_improvements: Removed 'to' from k8s_rbac_role_binding_to_service_account_only for better alignment with naming conventions
title: Access Enforcement - Access enforcement | role - based access control enforce a role-based access con
step1_reasoning: The control AC-3(7) focuses on enforcing role-based access control, which directly maps to Kubernetes RBAC. In Kubernetes, this is achieved through the use of Roles, ClusterRoles, RoleBindings, and ClusterRoleBindings. Ensuring that no ClusterRoleBinding grants cluster-admin privileges, enforcing least privilege, and ensuring RoleBindings are attached to ServiceAccounts are critical checks to ensure proper RBAC enforcement.
